question.text: Which of the following is a significance of the derivatives market?

answer.text: It facilitates price discovery and enables transfer of risk from risk-averse to risk-willing participants
It eliminates all types of financial risk from the market
It guarantees profits for all participants in the market
It replaces the need for cash markets in equities

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The derivatives market serves two key functions: (1) Price discovery — derivatives prices reflect market expectations about future prices, and (2) Risk transfer — derivatives allow hedgers to transfer risk to speculators who are willing to bear it. **The Trap:** Derivatives do not eliminate risk; they transfer risk from one party to another. They do not guarantee profits — many participants incur losses. Derivatives complement cash markets; they do not replace them. **Cross-Exam Context:** The significance of derivatives markets is tested in NISM Series VIII and other derivative exams. **Key Takeaway:** Two key functions: price discovery and risk transfer. Derivatives shift risk from those who want to avoid it (hedgers) to those willing to accept it (speculators).
